title: General Security Concepts
description: Foundational principles and the CIA triad.
points: Confidentiality protects information from unauthorized disclosure.
Integrity ensures data is not modified without authorization.
Availability keeps systems and data accessible when needed.
examTip: Remember the CIA triad for broad security questions.

labs.title: Phishing Investigation
labs.description: Inspect a suspicious email and identify indicators of compromise.
labs.outcome: Recognize phishing clues and response steps.

labs.title: Log Analysis
labs.description: Review generated system logs to identify anomalies.

title: Threats, Vulnerabilities & Mitigations
description: Common attack types and defensive measures.
points: Threats are potential events that exploit vulnerabilities.
Vulnerabilities are weaknesses in systems or processes.
Mitigations reduce risk through controls and best practices.
examTip: Map each threat to a likely mitigation.
commonMistake: Treating malware and phishing as the same category.

title: Security Architecture
points: Use segmentation and least privilege to reduce attack surface.
Apply encryption and strong authentication to protect resources.
Endpoint, network, and application security should be layered.
examTip: Favor layered defenses over single controls.
commonMistake: Assuming a firewall alone is sufficient.

title: Security Operations
description: Monitoring, detection, and incident response fundamentals.
points: Monitoring tools like SIEM help correlate events.
Incident response uses preparation, detection, containment, eradication, and recovery.
Logs are essential for analysis and auditing.
examTip: Know the order of incident response steps.
commonMistake: Skipping containment during an active incident.

title: Security Program Management & Oversight
points: Risk management balances business goals and security needs.
Policies, procedures, and awareness programs support compliance.
Security training reduces human error and strengthens culture.
examTip: Link governance to risk and compliance objectives.
commonMistake: Ignoring the human element in policy success.
